tools.name: semantic_search
tools.description: Search RedM/RDR3 docs by behavior, concept, OR exact token. Use when you don't have a specific native hash/name (use `lookup_native`) and the term isn't a known asset name in a large data table (use `grep_docs`). Hybrid mode (default) handles 'how do I X' queries ('teleport player', 'spawn vehicle',

tools.name: list_namespaces
tools.description: Orient yourself: list available doc categories and their namespaces. Use once at session start (or when unsure) before applying a `category=` / `namespace=` filter to `browse` / `semantic_search`. NOT a content search. Categories: `natives` (PLAYER, ENTITY, VEHICLE, â€¦), `vorp`, `rsgcore`, `oxmysql`,

tools.name: browse
tools.description: Enumerate doc paths in a category/namespace. Use to discover what exists before calling `get_document` or a targeted `grep_docs`. NOT a content search â€” use `semantic_search` for behavior/concept lookups or `grep_docs` for token lookups. Returns `{path, title, chunks}[]`.

tools.name: get_document
tools.description: Fetch full markdown of a doc by `path` (as returned by `browse`, `semantic_search`, or `grep_docs`). Use to retrieve full content after a search snippet looks promising. Pass `heading` (full breadcrumb like `Character Management > Inventory Management`, or just the leaf â€” case-insensitive, fuzzy) to

tools.name: read_lines
tools.description: Read an exact line range from a raw doc file by absolute line number â€” the windowed-read companion to `grep_docs`. When `grep_docs` returns a hit at `path:line` inside a large file, call `read_lines({ path, start, end })` to pull the surrounding block. This is the ONLY way to read around a hit in th

tools.name: grep_docs
tools.description: Find an EXACT literal token in raw doc files (markdown + lua). Use for specific weapon/ped/animation/prop/interior/zone names (`weapon_pistol_volcanic`, `a_c_bear_01`, `p_campfire01x`), known hashes (`0x020D13FF`), walkstyles/clipsets (`MP_Style_Casual`, `mech_loco_m@`), or any string you'd `grep` f

tools.name: lookup_native
tools.description: Resolve a RedM/RDR3 SCRIPT native by hash or name â€” O(1), exact. Use whenever you see `Citizen.InvokeNative(0x...)`, `Citizen.invokeNative('0x...')`, `GetHashKey('NAME')`, or a SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE native name (e.g. `SET_ENTITY_COORDS`, `GetPedHealth`) in Lua/JS/TS. NOT for game-data hashes (weapon/

tools.name: asset_lookup
tools.description: Resolve a RedM game-data asset (ped model, weapon, object, door, vehicle) by exact name, 32-bit hash, or partial-name search. O(1) structured lookup against pre-parsed discoveries tables â€” replaces the common workflow of grepping `a_c_bear_01` in peds_list.lua, then cross-referencing RELATIONSHIP/RE

tools.name: share_finding
tools.description: Share a verified finding back to the docs corpus so the next agent can find it. Use AFTER solving a non-trivial problem to record what would have saved you time: a gotcha, a working parameter combo, an undocumented constraint, a relationship between two natives that isn't obvious. Other agents will

tools.name: get_invoke_guide
tools.description: Load the calling-convention reference for RedM/RDR3 natives in `js` or `lua`. Call ONCE per session before writing native-calling code â€” every native doc page only shows Lua examples, so JS/TS authors need this to translate correctly. Covers result modifiers (`Citizen.resultAsInteger/Float/String/Ve

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "browse" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Enumerate doc paths in a category/namespace. Use to discover what exists before calling `get_document` or a targeted `grep_docs`. NOT a content search â€” use `seâ€¦

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "browse" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: list_namespaces, lookup_native Â· Private data: semantic_search, browse, get_document, read_lines, grep_docs, asset_lookup, share_finding Â· Public sink: share_finding Â· Destructive: lookup_native.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: list_namespaces, lookup_native Â· Private data: semantic_search, browse, get_document, read_lines, grep_docs, asset_lookup, share_finding Â· Public sink: share_finding Â· Destructive: lookup_native

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
